title: C4ADS exposes Chinese broker enabling DPRK tanker conversions for sanctions evasion

contents:
# C4ADS exposes Chinese broker enabling DPRK tanker conversions for sanctions evasion

## Overview
Open-source investigators at C4ADS revealed a Chinese maritime broker converting sanctioned North Korean tankers into dark fleet vessels. The broker altered vessel identities to dodge tracking. This supports DPRK's coal and arms export networks.

## Details
Broker identified as Dalian Sea Legend International Shipping, linked to refitting the Andaman (ex-DPRK tanker) with fake AIS signals. Operations occurred at Dalian shipyards in late 2025. Related vessels include Prestige Way and New Konk. C4ADS tracked 12 such conversions since 2024. UN Panel of Experts cited similar tactics in recent report. "China-based enablers remain key to DPRK's evasion," per C4ADS analyst. No direct casualties; focuses on proliferation finance.

**Location**: Dalian, China

## Source Context
The information comes from Twitter @C4ADS, which covers C4ADS sanctions evasion North Korea China dark fleet. The reporting matches their typical area of focus.

## Significance
This development adds to the ongoing pattern of activity tracked through this source. Analysts following C4ADS sanctions evasion North Korea China dark fleet will likely watch for follow-up reporting.
